item.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
item.description: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

item.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
item.description: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

item.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
item.description: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

item.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
item.description: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

item.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
item.description: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

item.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
item.description: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

item.title: Three youths charged with arson after fire at Ashley Youth Detention Centre in northern Tasmania
item.description: Police said they were called to the site on Sunday afternoon following reports of a disturbance involving three armed youths.

item.title: More accommodation desperately needed in Rockhampton and Yeppoon before 2032 Olympic Games
item.description: More than half of Queensland has rental vacancy rates of less than 1 per cent, and experts say closed and abandoned properties may be the solution — especially with the 2032 Olympic Games looming.

item.title: Australian world champion Matthew Hauser wins Noosa Triathlon
item.description: Only a fortnight after being crowned men's world champion, Australia's Matthew Hauser tops the podium at the revered Noosa Triathlon.

item.title: Former MP Nick Dametto declares victory in Townsville mayoral by-election
item.description: Nick Dametto, a former Queensland MP, has won 61 per cent of the votes counted, succeeding in his campaign to become Townsville's next mayor.

item.title: LNP's Wayde Chiesa promises to get to work after victory in Hinchinbrook by-election
item.description: The LNP's Wayde Chiesa will be the next representative for the Queensland seat of Hinchinbrook, which had been safely held by Katter's Australian Party since 2017.

item.title: Ashleigh Gentle shares 'incredibly isolating' miscarriage news before competing in T100 Qatar
item.description: Australian triathlete Ashleigh Gentle reveals she suffered a miscarriage just days before competing in the season-ending T100 event in Qatar, earning significant praise for her bravery.

item.title: Labor faces green 'Grinch' hospital funding accusations with a budget in the red
item.description: Labor has two options when it comes to straddling a budget in the red and accusations it has become a green Grinch on health funding.

item.title: The making of Handel's Messiah into a centuries-old Christmas classic
item.description: Composed in just three weeks, Handel’s Messiah remains a beloved classic nearly 300 years on. Its origin story is marked by scandal and links to slavery.

item.title: Analogue camera technicians with decades of experience retiring amid boom in film photography
item.description: As film photography surges in popularity among younger generations, technicians who repair analogue cameras are retiring, raising concerns it will become a dying art.

item.title: Geraldton's Horizon Ball sculpture draws large crowds as international visitor numbers rise
item.description: Geraldton's Horizon Ball sculpture is drawing huge crowds of tourists after the spot gained international attention on social media.

item.title: CLP announces Anjan Paudel as Nightcliff by-election candidate, Phil Scott runs as independent
item.description: More candidates for the Nightcliff by-election are announced, with Phil Scott running as an independent and the Country Liberal Party preselecting Anjan Paudel, who has stumbled while answering basic policy questions.

item.title: Disposable cameras in focus for Perth photography project
item.description: A unique community exhibition in Perth is celebrating the "messiness and spontaneity" of the disposable camera.

item.title: Perth doctor Rhys Bellinge's 'entitled, callous' behaviour killed Elizabeth Pearce and ruined lives, court told
item.description: Sought-after obstetrician Rhys Bellinge appeared to have it all — but that changed in an instant when his drunken, high-speed driving killed a young university graduate and left a rideshare driver with permanent injuries.

item.title: Lauren Parker to become dual Paralympian at Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games
item.description: Lauren Parker, who won gold as a triathlete at Paris 2024, will compete in two events in Milano Cortina 2026, less than a year after skiing for the first time.

item.title: Guide to Listening: ABC Classic's Festival of Female Composers and International Women's Day on ABC Jazz
item.description: For 10 years, in the lead-up to International Women's Day, ABC Classic has celebrated classical music by women across centuries. Here's what's in store for 2026.

item.title: Backlash deepens over AI seatbelt cameras as drivers fined for passengers’ behaviour
item.description: Frustration is deepening over drivers being ordered to pay hundreds of dollars in fines for infringements incurred by their passengers — including young children and neurodivergent people not wearing seatbelts correctly.

item.title: Nightcliff by-election will be a test for Territory Labor. Can it win it back?
item.description: Territory Labor lost the previously safe seat of Nightcliff as it was bundled out of government. Eighteen months on, does it deserve to win the seat back?

item.title: Can the Coalition hold onto Sussan Ley’s former seat of Farrer?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Tasmanian nurses say lower wages, understaffing a barrier to returning home
item.description: Tasmania needs more nurses, but lower wages and fewer opportunities for career growth remain barriers for enticing health workers to return to their home state.

item.title: Man wins seatbelt fine court battle as AI traffic cameras face scrutiny
item.description: A Queensland court decision casts fresh doubt on the reliability of controversial AI road-safety cameras after clearing a driver fined when his passenger moved their seatbelt during a trip.

item.title: Teen photographer Casey Jacobs puts focus on life in rural WA
item.description: Narrogin local Casey Jacobs is on a mission to capture agricultural landscapes across the Great Southern and share the stories of life on the land.

item.title: One Nation selects agricultural businessman David Farley to contest Farrer by-election in NSW
item.description: David Farley, 69, defeats two other candidates in today's preselection battle at the Albury Entertainment Centre.

item.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
item.description: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

item.title: Sheep stuck in flood waters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian Medical Association speaks out after top doctor suspended from Albury Wodonga Health
item.description: The Australian Medical Association is reviewing why several top doctors and nurses have left Albury Wodonga hospital.

item.title: More than 100 doctors file no-confidence vote in regional health service executive management
item.description: Two top doctors and a nurse have left Albury Wodonga hospital in just six months, prompting a vote of no confidence in management.

item.title: Road users encounter stray cattle on rural roads in the Western Australia's Great Southern.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Who is Anna Lapwood, the content creator bringing organ music to millions on social media
item.description: Anna Lapwood uses social media to share her love of the organ and favourite music from Bach to Lord of the Rings. Millions follow her and thousands more come to her concerts.

item.title: China cracks down on illegal fentanyl trade after Trump delays Beijing meeting
item.description: The country is one of the world’s highest producers of the chemicals used to produce the synthetic opioid in America.

item.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
item.description: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

item.title: Donald Trump orders ICE agents to man US airport security checkpoints
item.description: A fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security leads to lengthy queues at US airports.

item.title: Illegal e-bike riders fined in Sydney's south as police warn of further enforcement
item.description: More than 300 e-bike riders have been stopped, including e-scooter riders, during an ongoing police operation in the Sutherland Shire.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Mannix starts Mums WhatsApp group, Wallam's revenge and Housby back to her best
item.description: Emily Mannix talks about her extended absence from the Super Netball league, and why she's now ready to return for the Melbourne Vixens after a "freak accident" meant her daughter Mabel needed emergency care at birth.

item.title: State library showcases photo archive of trailblazing Indigenous journalist Wayne Coolwell
item.description: Journalist and broadcaster Wayne Coolwell documented years of Indigenous culture with his 35mm camera. The photographs are going on showcase at the State Library.

item.title: WA tourist towns grapple with wildlife loss, damage to businesses after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

item.title: Australia takes 2-0 lead in three-game ODI series against West Indies at Warner Park
item.description: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

item.title: More than 200 buildings damaged in Exmouth, residents stranded as airport, roads remain closed
item.description: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

item.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi half-century leads Rajasthan Royals to IPL win over Chennai Super Kings
item.description: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes a 15-ball half-century as he leads Rajasthan Royals to an easy victory over Chennai Super Kings.

item.title: Australians reveal toll of fuel price rises on their lives as cost of living goes up
item.description: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

item.title: England's Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell let off with warning over New Zealand nightclub altercation
item.description: Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell escape with a caution notice following an investigation into a nightclub incident in ‌New Zealand late ⁠last year.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Hurry up, I don't have all day to watch you struggle at internet trivia
item.description: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland coast
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

item.title: Jason Day sad to see Tiger Woods struggle with addiction, miss Masters after DUI charge
item.description: Australian number two Jason Day says Tiger Woods's struggles are a reminder that anyone can struggle with addiction, but admits it was "selfish" for the golf great to get behind the wheel.

item.title: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner facing drink driving charges in Sydney
item.description: Former Australian Test cricketer David Warner is charged with mid-range drink driving in Sydney's east over the weekend.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 8: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip
item.description: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

item.title: 'Ketamine Queen' jailed for 15 years over death of Friends star Matthew Perry
item.description: Jayvee Sangha admits to running a "stash house" that helped supply the famous Friends star ketamine in the months before his death.

item.title: Nick Faldo says he will not comment on rival Greg Norman until after US Masters
item.description: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

item.title: Regular unleaded petrol to peak in May — as it happened
item.description: Westpac has drawn a line in the sand and said unleaded petrol prices are projected to peak at $2.46 a litre in late May, with a 26 cents increase when excise returns to normal. Look back at how the trading session unfolded with our specialist business reporters.

item.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant contributing factor' in Canberra teenager's drowning death, coroner finds
item.description: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

item.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed three-year-old girl
item.description: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

item.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
item.description: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me
item.description: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

item.title: Cameron Young, Jason Day apply pressure to Rory McIlroy in third round of Masters
item.description: It will not be a procession to a second straight Masters title for Rory McIlroy, after a tense third round at Augusta National.

item.title: Love story: When love means donating a kidney to your brother
item.description: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

item.title: How these queer couples divide the mental load in their household
item.description: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

item.title: Pop culture quiz: Can you tell the difference between The Pitt and ER?
item.description: Ahead of The Pitt season finale dropping this week, can you pick the difference between it and its hospital drama ancestor ER?

item.title: Alex de Minaur loses to Hamad Medjedovic at Barcelona Open
item.description: Alex de Minaur's uneven season has hit another road bump as Australia's number one succumbed to a shock last-16 loss at the Barcelona Open.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through orchestral music in Adelaide
item.description: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

item.title: Liberal candidate under investigation for roadworks ahead of Nepean by-election
item.description: The Liberal Party's candidate for the crucial upcoming Nepean by-election is being investigated by Victoria's transport department for unauthorised roadworks in a campaign stunt with Opposition Leader Jess Wilson.

item.title: World's largest live export ship sails out of Darwin with 17,000 cattle bound for Indonesia
item.description: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

item.title: Australia's 'lucky country' mantle has slipped — and we're not happy about it
item.description: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Sydney kids turn struggle with school into ambitious art show
item.description: At an art gallery in Sydney's inner west, a group of marginalised children are staging their first exhibition — and for many of them, it is the first time they have felt seen.

item.title: ASX edges higher despite rising Hormuz tension — as it happened
item.description: The ASX 200 clawed back earlier, steeper losses to close marginally higher with demand for retailers and banks outweighing selling in energy stocks and miners.

item.title: Last chance for answers in Nannup Four cold case as police admit search error
item.description: The family of a mother and daughter who went missing, along with two others, in WA almost 20 years ago are pinning their hopes on a last-ditch effort to uncover the truth of what became of them.

item.title: About 2,000 fines withdrawn since AI road safety cameras introduced in WA
item.description: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

item.title: China opens the gate to more Aussie beef with new export licences for several facilities
item.description: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

item.title: Expectant parents and ED patients diverted as two regional hospitals struggle with workforce shortages
item.description: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

item.title: Adelaide Biennial of Australian Art sees how 24 artists working across the country Yield Strength
item.description: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

item.title: Australian golf junior hoping to test his skills at Canadian invitational
item.description: Alby Weyden was two years old when he became obsessed with golf. Four years on, his family hopes to raise enough money for him to compete in the Canadian Invitational.

item.title: A family's generational Anzac legacy and fight for compensation
item.description: No family in the world comes close to the fighting dynasty of Ricky Morris's. So, why are they still denied land owed generations ago?

item.title: Mitch Marsh 50 not enough as Lucknow Super Giants fall to fourth straight Indian Premier League defeat
item.description: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina hits world ranking low after early Madrid Open exit
item.description: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

item.title: Advocates call for improved medical resourcing at prisons after a coronial inquest into the death of a prisoner
item.description: Advocates call for improved medical resourcing in South Australia's prisons after a coronial inquest into the death of an inmate.

item.title: Leading Trump envoy asks FIFA to replace Iran with Italy at World Cup amid Pope Leo XIV attacks
item.description: As the US president feuds with Pope Leo, one of his top envoys suggests giving Italy a World Cup spot could mend fences.

item.title: Fuel prices in Australia: how rising costs are forcing Australians to change their habits
item.description: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

item.title: Italy dismisses Trump official's 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup
item.description: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

item.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
item.description: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

item.title: Defamation trial shown messages between Rebel Wilson and film producer
item.description: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

item.title: World War II veterans meet for first time eight decades after Borneo service
item.description: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

item.title: US Justice Department drops criminal investigation of Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell
item.description: The move could lead to a swift confirmation vote by the Senate for Kevin Warsh, a former top Federal Reserve official whom President Donald Trump nominated in January to replace Mr Powell.

item.title: This easy 'hug in a bowl' chicken stew is perfect for midweek dinners - ABC News
item.description: "It's a shortcut to that feeling that you get when you've slow cooked something all Sunday, but without that time or labour."

item.title: Thousands attend Anzac Day services across Perth and WA, a few moved on for booing
item.description: Leaders have condemned "cretins" who interjected and booed at Perth and Western Australian Anzac Day ceremonies, with the premier labelling their actions "disgusting and disrespectful".

item.title: Cotton industry 'alert but not alarmed' by increasing numbers of bicoloured weevils in NT crops
item.description: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

item.title: How Henry the dachshund puppy survived three months lost in Perth's southern suburbs
item.description: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

item.title: Donald Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as Middle East peace hopes fade
item.description: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Calling all word wizards. Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

item.title: Long-held bitumen standards waived after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

item.title: Indigenous kids training for life in and out of the ring at youth development camp in Canberra
item.description: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

item.title: Why Shaun Micallef wrote 'gothic romance novel' De'Ath Takes a Holiday
item.description: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

item.title: Resources industry in the spotlight during Angus Taylor's first trip to WA as Opposition leader
item.description: This past week marks the first time the Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Taylor and Premier Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

item.title: Daniel MacPherson and Amy Shark fight for their lives in Beast
item.description: Bosch & Rockit filmmaker Tyler Atkins's latest feature put his stars through the wringer on camera, but held them with love behind the scenes.

item.title: Why street artist Peter Drew decided to bring back his 'Aussie' poster series
item.description: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

item.title: Canberra is growing its economic 'pie' to become more than a city of public administration
item.description: The capital has long been known as a public service town, but with about two in three workers now employed in the private sector and the population expected to reach nearly 700,000 by 2050, is that reputation — and key economic driver — likely to change?

item.title: Influencers to pay $100 to make money off Tasmania's national parks under new permit system
item.description: Influencers and content creators will be able to film and profit from content gathered in Tasmania's national parks for just $100, under new regulations.

item.title: McDonald's to set up Northcote store after overturning council ruling
item.description: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

item.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it
item.description: Sabastian Sawe smashes one of athletics' seemingly unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

item.title: Sheep farmers swear by Lifetime Ewe course to steer them through drought and diesel price spikes
item.description: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

item.title: Kerry Kennedy thought of father's assassination when shooter rushed security at gala dinner
item.description: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

item.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
item.description: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman's court battle to reveal ongoing power struggle at Open AI
item.description: The world's richest man is accusing the company and its chief executive Sam Altman, and Microsoft of betraying the AI start up's original mission.

item.title: The best ways to protect credit and debit cards from fraud
item.description: Experts reveal the most common ways fraudsters steal card details and how to keep your accounts safer.

item.title: The security breach that exposed Donald Trump to a shooting incident
item.description: A shooting incident at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has prompted a swirl of questions about how an alleged shooter breached Secret Service security and got so close to the US president.

item.title: Learn why tomorrow's inflation data could be important for you in just three minutes
item.description: There are some key moments coming up for the Australian economy and they could make a big difference to your bank account. Emily Stewart — aka Sensible Emily — is here to help you understand what to look out for.

item.title: Doctors concerned about $3.2b new Women’s and Children’s Hospital design
item.description: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns", including the location of the women's assessment service.

item.title: Suspect in Washington dinner shooting charged with trying to assassinate Donald Trump
item.description: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

item.title: What I've learnt about living on other mobs' Country — respectfully
item.description: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

item.title: Dolphin spotted in Melbourne's Yarra River probably hunting prey, researcher says
item.description: Researchers say the dolphin appears healthy and is most likely chasing prey in the Melbourne waterway.

item.title: Locust swarms in the Murtho area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Locusts seen on Riverland farm
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Worried about a neighbour's dog? Here's what you can do
item.description: An unhappy dog doesn't necessarily mean criminal neglect, even if the level of care is below what you would give your own pet.

item.title: Queenslanders urged to get tested for viral hepatitis after liver cancer deaths triple since 1980s
item.description: An alarming new report has found liver cancer deaths have risen from 1.6 deaths per 100,000 people in the 1980s to 5.1 per 100,000 in 2018–2022.

item.title: Parents slam 'weak' response at The Friends' School after girls targeted in AI deepfake pornography scandal
item.description: Two parents of girls at a private school have criticised the institution's response to a deepfake incident, saying they were encouraged not to tell their daughters they had been identified in material shared in a boys' group chat.

item.title: El Niño warming could be unprecedented, but don't assume record drought in Australia
item.description: Australia's weather pattern is in a reset, and the weather events happening this month will act like the ingredients to determine the flavour of our climate for the rest of the year.

item.title: Lions star Oscar Allen sidelined for 12-14 weeks with foot injury
item.description: The Lions' win over the Crows on Sunday has come at a price, with one of their major off-season signings rule out for much of the season.

item.title: UK parliament votes against inquiry into Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over Peter Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer criticised the vote, calling it a political stunt by the Conservative Party timed to sway voters ahead of local and regional elections on May 7.

item.title: The search for missing girl Sharon Granites
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ultra-swimmer Andy Donaldson sets world record with 55km Ord River challenge
item.description: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

item.title: Australia's richest person donates 'sexy' $1 million plane to Pauline Hanson's One Nation
item.description: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
item.description: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

item.title: FIFA allows Afghan women's football team to return to international competition
item.description: Afghan women footballers will be able to showcase their skills to the world for the first time in five years after FIFA cleared the way for their return to international competition.

item.title: Threat to homes eases as fire near Uralla in NSW downgraded
item.description: The threat posed to homes by a grass fire burning on the state's Northern Tablelands eases after earlier prompting an Emergency alert.

item.title: SA Legislative Council result delay creating 'confusion and uncertainty'
item.description: With less than a week until state parliament resumes, an incoming upper house member says she still cannot access the building or appoint staff because the election result is yet to be declared.

item.title: APRA music awards 2026 winners announced, with Amyl and the Sniffers taking top gong
item.description: Punk rockers Amyl and The Sniffers have bagged three top gongs at the Australian music industry's APRA awards.

item.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
item.description: The ruling will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow and by the Republican party as a boost ahead of key midterm elections in November.

item.title: Jeff Buckley doco It's Never Over humanises the man beyond the tortured myth
item.description: The otherworldly singer-guitarist died at 30 and struggled with fame. His guitarist and a new movie honour Buckley's humanity and musical genius.

item.title: King Charles III visits September 11 memorial as he tours New York
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

item.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
item.description: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

item.title: Anastasia Potapova reaches Madrid Open semifinals to make WTA Tour history
item.description: Anastasia Potapova experiences an "explosion of emotions", as her remarkable run in Madrid continues at the prestigious tournament.

item.title: Six WA Police officers sanctioned over treatment of Aboriginal man who died in Kalgoorlie prison
item.description: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed at news of his death.

item.title: Donald Trump's would-be assassin took hotel selfie while 'armed to the teeth', court told
item.description: Court documents reveal further details about the case against the alleged gunman at the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington.

item.title: Does the 'Jessica' tantrum trend really work?
item.description: Whether it's at home or in the middle of a shopping centre, tantrums can test the patience of any parent or caregiver.

item.title: Fuel crisis forces regional councils to delay construction and road maintenance
item.description: Regional roadworks and construction projects are being put on hold as Victorian councils tighten their budgets amid the international fuel crisis.

item.title: Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion interim report delivers 14 recommendations
item.description: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion releases its interim report, making 14 recommendations.

item.title: Mark Latham ordered to pay Alex Greenwich $100k and delete posts in vilification case ruling
item.description: The former One Nation MP has been found to have vilified openly gay politician Alex Greenwich in the second case centring around a dispute between the pair.

item.title: Archibald Packing Room Prize 2026 won by Sean Layh for portrait of Jacob Collins
item.description: Sean Layh has won the $3,000 Packing Room Prize at this year's Archibald Prize for his portrait of Jacob Collins, titled The tragicall historie of Hamlet, Prince of Denmarke.

item.title: Miranda Priestly makes a splashy return in The Devil Wears Prada 2
item.description: The Devil Wears Prada 2 drops the beloved (or loathed) characters from 20 years ago into a very different media environment.

item.title: What is inflation and should you worry about it?
item.description: The latest inflation data is making headlines. Find out what it means for you in three minutes.

item.title: Counterterrorism intelligence's share of funding in 'significant' decline since 2020, royal commission finds
item.description: Royal commissioner Virginia Bell says classified documents reveal a significant shift in intelligence spending away from counterterrorism since 2020, in the first findings of what failings led to last year's Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Tense exchanges in court as judge turns screws on ACCC's case against Woolworths
item.description: There have been tense exchanges in the Federal Court, with the judge overseeing the bombshell case between the ACCC and Woolworths questioning the consumer watchdog's case.

item.title: Crows' skipper Jordan Dawson ruled out for Showdown after attending brother's funeral
item.description: Adelaide Crow's skipper Jordan Dawson has been ruled out of Friday's Showdown following his brother's sudden death.

item.title: Western Queensland's Pilungah Reserve third to gain special status ending mineral prospecting permits
item.description: Pilungah Reserve, south-west of Mount Isa, has become Queensland's third special wildlife reserve, barring any future mining activity.

item.title: Rebel Wilson says social media posts are true during defamation trial cross-examination
item.description: Rebel Wilson tells a court several social media posts targeting the lead actor on her film The Deb are all true, on her final day of cross-examination in a defamation trial.

item.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
item.description: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

item.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
item.description: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

item.title: SA premier compares course upgrade for in-doubt LIV Golf with similar AFL Gather Round investments
item.description: Peter Malinauskas compares spending $45 million to redevelop the North Adelaide golf course to similar funding for the AFL to play in the Barossa Valley, as media reports suggest Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is preparing to pull financial support for LIV Golf.

item.title: Victorian bottle shops awash with illicit alcohol, researchers find
item.description: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

item.title: Man accused of attending Mount Gambier businesses while nude faces court
item.description: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

item.title: NSW strikes late to win Game I as Origin series heads to Queensland
item.description: NSW has come from behind to win the opening match of the Women's State of Origin series, and their coach is looking forward to trying to win the shield in Queensland.

item.title: Moore goals after siren as Hawks and Pies settle for thrilling draw
item.description: A goal after the siren from Dylan Moore allows Hawthorn to claim a draw against Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: NRL coach Craig Bellamy diagnosed with form of neurodegenerative disorder
item.description: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy has been diagnosed with a form of neurodegenerative disorder, the club has said.

item.title: Community in mourning after 5yo girl's body found
item.description: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

item.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Alice Springs girl Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

item.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

item.title: How two 'Origin plays' turned the tide for New South Wales against Queensland
item.description: With Queensland on the verge of another classic ambush, it took two "Origin plays" for New South Wales to turn the tide in the women's series opener in Newcastle.

item.title: Live: Alleged killer of 5yo girl moved to Darwin after violent clashes outside hospital
item.description: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following after the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

item.title: Guy Montgomery went from lazy school student to hosting a TV spelling bee across two countries
item.description: Guy Montgomery is back with the third season of his inimitable Spelling Bee, with brand-new sidekick Sam Campbell. We turn the tables on the Kiwi comic to ask him the tough questions.

item.title: Seasonal produce for all your autumn soups, roasts and salads
item.description: From carrots to cauliflower, here is some of the best seasonal produce to enjoy in your autumn soups, roasts and salads.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A Trump shooting scare, a stunt gone wrong, a burger blue and a rescued roo
item.description: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

item.title: Bond yields at crisis level as Australian government's 10-Year Treasury rate passes 5 per cent
item.description: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, pushes past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

item.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
item.description: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

item.title: ACCC case against Woolworths winds up as spotlight remains on integrity of grocery pricing
item.description: As the Woolies federal court case wraps up and the Coles matter awaits judgment, here's what we've learned so far from the high-stakes cases.

item.title: In a normal political world, the Liberal Party would be expecting a Nepean by-election win
item.description: With no Labor candidate contesting this weekend's Nepean by-election, it should be a cakewalk for the Victorian Liberals. But we are no longer in a normal political world.

item.title: Cole Tomas Allen to remain in jail after alleged attempt to storm correspondents' dinner
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, appears briefly in court, days after he allegedly attempted to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner armed with guns and knives.

item.title: The Bondi royal commission interim report is careful but ultimately damning
item.description: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

item.title: Live: Iran threatens 'long and painful strikes' if US renews attacks
item.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

item.title: Impact of Queensland's coercive control laws not yet clear one year after implementation
item.description: When Stephanie* called the police to report coercive control, she says she was told it was "too hard too prove" and it was "unlikely a case would ever succeed".

item.title: New photo of Aung San Suu Kyi released as military says she is moved from prison to house arrest
item.description: Myanmar's detained former president Aung San Suu Kyi has been moved from prison to house arrest, according to state media.

item.title: National average house prices rose slightly in April, the slowest pace of growth since January 2025
item.description: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

item.title: China using a double-insurance strategy to secure crude oil supplies amid Iran war
item.description: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Violence erupts as rioters clash with police outside Alice Springs Hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pacific nations aim to tackle regional unrest with expanded Pacific Response Group
item.description: The Pacific Response Group is beginning negotiations on a treaty that would allow it to respond more quickly to natural disasters and other crises across the region.

item.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone' but regional South Australians are grappling with a changing environment
item.description: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

item.title: 'No secrets': Alternative school's history of discipline in spotlight
item.description: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

item.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says
item.description: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

item.title: Once-safe Coalition seat Farrer now in sights of One Nation at by-election
item.description: A fascinating four-cornered contest is brewing as voters in the southern NSW seat of Farrer head to the ballot box for the second time in 12 months.

item.title: Former Canberra surgeon to face disciplinary action for perjury, attempting to pervert the course of justice
item.description: Former Canberra vascular surgeon Imran Kader will face disciplinary action after he lied under oath and tried to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.

item.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
item.description: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

item.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis
item.description: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

item.title: Canadian authorities search for missing Australian hiker Denise Ann Williams in Nova Scotia
item.description: Canadian police are searching for a Victorian woman who disappeared while hiking on an island on the country's east coast.

item.title: Live: Taylor says government is in 'denial' about situation in NT town camps
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says the federal government is in "denial" about the situation in town camps following the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby. Follow live.

item.title: Live: Wall Street surges despite Meta and Microsoft dives and ASX set for strong rebound
item.description: Wall Street rises despite Meta crashing on AI spending fears. The ASX is poised for a strong rebound after seven consecutive losing sessions. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Extreme heat the most deadly yet preventable natural disaster, NSW Net Zero Commission says
item.description: An agency set up by the NSW government just two years ago says extreme heat is the most deadly yet most preventable natural hazard facing Australians.

item.title: Josh Hazlewood smashed for 24 off one over as Shubman Gill fires in the IPL
item.description: Shubman Gill takes Australian quick Josh Hazlewood to task as Gujarat took down Bengaluru in the IPL.

item.title: Emma Thompson stars in a new film made for an orchestral performance of Holst’s The Planets
item.description: A new silent film starring Emma Thompson and Greg Wise will accompany the Sydney Symphony Orchestra and Holst’s The Planets in a world-premiere performance.

item.title: Turning Strelley Station into a community was hard work.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Left in the red dirt: The forgotten trail of Australia's longest strike
item.description: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

item.title: Sea creatures, Anzac Day and AI camera woes feature in top WA photo picks for April
item.description: From dawn services to record-breaking swims, here are our top photo picks for Western Australia in April.

item.title: Police raid apartment owned by Vahid Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk
item.description: Police have raided a Gold Coast apartment owned by the partner of former Queensland Premier Annastacia Palaszczuk.

item.title: US Congress approves bill to end record Homeland Security funding shutdown
item.description: "It is about damn time," said one Democrat as US politicians voted to restore funding to most of the department's agencies after Mr Trump's deadly immigration crackdown in Minneapolis.

item.title: Remote patient monitoring technology saving time, travel and money for regional mums during pregnancy
item.description: Remote health technology is saving regional patients time and money, despite many Victorian hospitals still running on paper records.

item.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs in Ventura, California
item.description: Britney Spears has been charged in California with driving under the influence of drugs and alcohol.

item.title: Busselton triathlon festival hits 25-year milestone amid continued growth of endurance sports
item.description: With thousands of competitors set to flood Busselton for its annual Festival of Triathlon, the tourist town has become a key stop on Australia's endurance sport circuit.
